headline: Hezbollah Breach, Israeli Night Raid Escalate West Bank Amid Iranian Cyber Chaos
subheadline: IDF seized over 250 weapons and arrested 19 suspects in Bethlehem, while Hezbollah militants engaged Israeli forces on Al‑Ta'ar ridge using a bulldozer and motorcycle.
lede: The Israeli Defense Forces conducted a night raid in Bethlehem, arresting 19 suspects and confiscating more than 250 weapons and 40 weapon‑making kits. Hezbollah fighters then attempted to breach the Al‑Ta'ar ridge, approaching Israeli positions in a bulldozer and motorcycle before being killed by lethal fire. Simultaneously, Iran’s banking and payment systems suffered widespread cyber disruptions, leaving ATMs and internet banking largely offline. In the east, Russian tactical aviation dropped guided bombs across four Ukrainian regions while Ukrainian forces reported over a thousand UAV and air attacks on settlements, including a missile strike that killed three civilians in Kryvyi Rih.

sections.title: Russian Air Strikes Intensify Ukrainian Front
sections.body: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs across Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions. Ukrainian authorities recorded 1,050 attacks on 56 settlements in Zaporizhzhia, including 769 UAVs and 18 airstrikes in a single day. UAV groups from northern Sumy moved toward Chernihiv, targeting villages such as Ponornitsya and Holmy, while additional UAV sightings were reported in Poltava, Dnipropetrovsk and Cherkasy. A Russian missile strike in Kryvyi Rih killed three civilians and wounded 23, and threats of Iskander‑M and S‑300 launches were announced from Voronezh, Kursk and Crimea.

sections.title: Israel Expands Operations as Iran Faces Cyber Turmoil
sections.body: The IDF’s night operation in Bethlehem arrested 19 wanted individuals and seized more than 250 weapons and 40 weapon‑making kits. Hezbollah militants crossed the security line on Al‑Ta'ar ridge, approached Israeli forces on a bulldozer and motorcycle, and were engaged with lethal fire. Iranian banks are under severe cyber attacks, crippling POS devices, ATMs and internet banking services nationwide. U.S. carrier strike groups Lincoln and Bush remain positioned in the Arabian Sea, while Prime Minister Netanyahu called for reduced reliance on U.S. arms and development of independent Israeli weapons production.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: Hezbollah’s use of civilian‑cover vehicles on the Al‑Ta'ar ridge signals a growing willingness to exploit border vulnerabilities, raising direct threats to Israeli forces. Iran’s internal cyber disruption of its banking sector could spill into broader regional cyber conflict, while the continued presence of U.S. carrier groups sustains deterrence but heightens escalation risk.
